items.name: Grimy harralander
items.examine: A mid-range herb wearing most of the garden it grew in.

items.name: Harralander
items.examine: Clean at last. It hums a steady, useful sort of note.

items.name: Grimy toadflax
items.examine: Neither toad nor flax, and currently mostly mud.

items.name: Toadflax
items.examine: A steadying herb. The Sustainers swear by it; the toads stay neutral.

items.name: Grimy avantoe
items.examine: Under all that dirt there is, allegedly, a herb.

items.name: Avantoe
items.examine: Smells faintly of rivers and long walks you no longer have to take.

items.name: Grimy kwuarm
items.examine: It bit the last person who washed it carelessly.

items.name: Kwuarm
items.examine: A pugnacious herb. Brews that want a fight start here.

items.name: Grimy cadantine
items.examine: Stubborn dirt on a stubborn herb. Fitting.

items.name: Cadantine
items.examine: Tough as old boots and far better for your skin.

items.name: Grimy dwarf weed
items.examine: Short, filthy, and worth more than it looks. No relation.

items.name: Dwarf weed
items.examine: Small herb, long reach. Archers pay handsomely for the irony.

items.name: Grimy truechord bloom
items.examine: Even caked in soil it rings faintly in the key of growing things.

items.name: Truechord bloom
items.examine: The Greenchord's own flower. Hold it to your ear: a perfect, patient note.

items.name: Harralander seed
items.examine: A held breath, waiting for soil.

items.name: Toadflax seed
items.examine: Plant it and stand back. It dislikes being watched.

items.name: Avantoe seed
items.examine: Tiny, expensive, and convinced of its own importance.

items.name: Kwuarm seed
items.examine: Handle with gloves. It practises biting early.

items.name: Cadantine seed
items.examine: The Blight wants this seed. Plant it out of spite.

items.name: Dwarf weed seed
items.examine: A small seed with a long grudge against poor soil.

items.name: Truechord seed
items.examine: The rarest note in the garden, folded up small. Plant true.

items.name: Onion seed
items.examine: Future tears, conveniently packaged.

items.name: Strawberry seed
items.examine: Sweetness on layaway.

items.name: Onion
items.examine: It has layers. So does your regret at biting it raw.

items.name: Strawberry
items.examine: Proof the Greenchord occasionally writes a pop song.

items.name: Limpwurt root
items.examine: A root with opinions about your upper body strength.

items.name: Red spiders' eggs
items.examine: The mother was not consulted. Do not ask twice.

items.name: White berries
items.examine: Pale, bitter, and excellent at keeping you in one piece.

items.name: Snape grass
items.examine: Grows by the troughs and smells of clean water and second chances.

items.name: Resonant dust
items.examine: Ground from ringing shards. It tunes a brew one octave sharper.

items.name: Strength potion
items.examine: Tastes like a dare. Your arms accept.

items.name: Restore potion
items.examine: Puts your levels back where you left them.

items.name: Energy potion
items.examine: Bottled second wind. The walk-weary's best friend.

items.name: Steadying brew
items.examine: Sword arm and shield arm, briefly in perfect harmony.

items.name: Antiblight tonic
items.examine: Sour to drink, sweet to survive. The Offnote hates the aftertaste.

items.name: Super strength potion
items.examine: The strength potion after a training montage.

items.name: Prayer renewal potion
items.examine: Like cold chapel air, but the deluxe vintage.

items.name: Super defence potion
items.examine: Drink it and watch swords reconsider.

items.name: Ranging potion
items.examine: Sharpens the eye. The arrows were always willing.

items.name: Super restore potion
items.examine: Everything back to true, plus a sip of grace for the soul.

items.name: Extreme attack potion
items.examine: A super attack potion, re-tuned until it sings sharp.

items.name: Extreme strength potion
items.examine: Strength with the safety filed off.

items.name: Truechord draught
items.examine: A full chord, bottled. Drink it and be, briefly, entirely in tune.

items.name: Compost
items.examine: Yesterday's crops, tomorrow's guarantee.

items.name: Supercompost
items.examine: Compost that went on to better things.

items.name: Verdant trowel
items.examine: A gardener's trowel with a Sustainer's polish. The soil approves.

items.name: Resonant dibber
items.examine: It hums when it meets good earth. Sometimes the seed stays in your hand.

items.name: Herb sack
items.examine: Smells of forty harvests. Roomier than it has any right to be.

items.name: Gardener's hat
items.examine: Wide-brimmed and weathered. The official halo of the Sustainers.

items.name: Gardener's smock
items.examine: Pockets full of seeds you don't remember pocketing.

items.name: Gardener's trousers
items.examine: Permanently green at the knees. A mark of rank.

items.name: Gardener's boots
items.examine: They have stood in things. They will stand in more.

objs.name: Compost bin
objs.examine: Where crops go to become a promise.

objs.name: Limpwurt plant
objs.examine: A scowling little plant guarding a valuable root.

objs.name: White berry bush
objs.examine: Pale berries on a patient bush.

objs.name: Snape grass
objs.examine: A clump of snape grass, thriving by the trough water.

npcs.name: Warden Brisa
npcs.examine: Keeper of the Seed Trough. Counts seeds the way bankers count coins.

npcs.name: Master Sustainer Oak
npcs.examine: Has kept the long green note true for sixty years. Hums when he listens.

npcs.name: Apothecary Vell
npcs.examine: Smells of forty herbs at once. Claims this is a professional credential.

npcs.name: Keeper Thistle
npcs.examine: Minds the Reward Trough. Prickly about it, naturally.

npcs.name: Blight Warden Mara
npcs.examine: Fights rot with a trowel and an unreasonable amount of optimism.

sharedEdits.what: Import the new Verdancourt intent module for its registration side effect (next to the other intent-* imports around line 39-44).
sharedEdits.snippet: import './intent-verdancourt'; // side-effect: registers verd-pick / verd-compost / verd-extreme domains

sharedEdits.what: Import the Verdancourt content pack for its side effects.
Expand the consume intent's DRINKABLE allow-list (line ~683) with every new potion id, or each Drink returns 'not drinkable'.

THE one real engine hook (spec verdict note 4): make the consume intent read data-driven effect keys from items.json, mirroring the restoresPrayer precedent. New potion items in this fragment already carry the data: `boosts: [{skill, amount}]` (transient +level combat boost), `restoreStats: SkillName[]` (clear lowered-stat debuffs back to base), `restoreEnergy: number` (run energy), `curesPoison: true`, plus the already-supported `restoresPrayer`. Store boosts in a transient state field (e.g. state.statBoosts: [{skill, amount, until}]) and add the delta where combat.ts derives lvl(state,'Attack'/'Strength'/'Defence'/'Ranged') (~lines 159-173), with a sim-tick expiry. Until this lands, prayer_renewal/super_restore prayer restore works via the existing restoresPrayer path; boost/restore/energy keys are inert data.
sharedEdits.snippet: const def = DRINK_ITEMS[item]; for (const b of def?.boosts ?? []) pushStatBoost(state, b.skill, b.amount, getSimTick() + 500);

sharedEdits.what: Compost application (spec verdict note 5): crafting compost/supercompost at Verdancourt's compost bins is live via the new 'verd-compost' intent, but APPLYING it needs a FarmPatch.compost field: farm-plant accepts an optional {compost: 'compost'|'supercompost'} payload consuming 1 from inventory; farm-harvest yields a fixed 5 (compost) or randInt(3,5)+1 (supercompost) instead of randInt(3,5). Also client content.ts farm-plant tool check hasTool('seed_dibber') should accept 'resonant_dibber' and 'verdant_trowel' as planting tools; resonant_dibber's no-seed-consumed chance (suggest 20%) is a farm-plant branch.
sharedEdits.snippet: const yieldN = patch.compost === 'compost' ? 5 : patch.compost === 'supercompost' ? randInt(3, 5) + 1 : randInt(3, 5);

sharedEdits.what: OPTIONAL (deferred): gardener outfit full-set +5% Farming xp bonus. The four gardeners_* pieces ship as ordinary armour; the set bonus needs a check in addXp for Farming when all four are equipped. Herb_sack likewise ships as a collectible until a container mechanic exists. Spec's 'Sustainer points' token currency was cut — the Reward Trough is a coin sink at reward-tier prices instead (no engine support for a second currency).
